Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly chronic kidney disease. Low total CO₂ may indicate metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals protein, glucose, ketones, and abnormal appearance, supporting renal dysfunction and possible glycosuria despite normal blood glucose and low HbA1c, which may reflect altered renal handling or analytical interference. Microalbumin/creatinine ratio is also highly elevated. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying etiology and complications.
